title: Decouple runtime refresh from Grabowski aggregate DB schema
goal: Replace Bureau runtime-refresh coupling to Grabowski’s aggregate resource-database schema with an explicit versioned lease-subsystem compatibility contract so unrelated additive Grabowski schema changes do not require a manual Bureau bootstrap while unknown lease semantics still fail closed.

acceptance.assertion: Grabowski exposes a stable, explicit resource-lease schema or capability version separate from its aggregate database schema, and Bureau validates that exact lease contract before reading lease rows.

A change to unrelated Grabowski tables does not block Bureau runtime refresh when the resource-lease contract is unchanged; tests reproduce the schema-1-to-schema-2 incident without a direct installer bootstrap.
Missing, future, corrupt or semantically incompatible resource-lease contract versions fail closed before runtime mutation with bounded recovery diagnostics.

The oldest supported installed Bureau runtime can safely promote a reviewed compatibility update across supported Grabowski schema transitions using the normal intent, exact lease, durable apply and immutable readback path.

Exact-head tests cover Bureau and Grabowski readers/writers, mixed versions, additive aggregate-schema changes and incompatible lease-schema changes; both repositories pass complete validation and CI.
A live promotion on copies of the production stores and then on the real runtime proves no manual direct-installer bypass is required, all exact leases are released, and runtime lag returns to zero.

relationship.GRABOWSKI-OPERATOR-SURFACE-V1-T062: Migrates Grabowski task and resource stores but does not define a consumer-specific lease compatibility contract for Bureau.

metadata.safety_boundary: Do not infer lease compatibility from table shape alone, accept arbitrary future aggregate database schemas, weaken owner/hash/expiry/path checks, or grant deployment authority.
does_not_establish: permission to mutate canonical dirty checkouts
